key_departures.impact: Robinson had his best professional season in 2025, finishing with 92 catches for a career-high 1,014 yards and four touchdowns as the team's primary slot receiver and No. 2 option behind Malik Nabers. He signed a 4-year, $78M deal with the Titans. His departure is a meaningful loss to the passing game, though the Giants did not pursue him aggressively and OC Matt Nagy prefers a bigger slot receiver profile.

Flott had his best season in 2025 — 14 starts, 11 passes defended, 1 INT, 3 TFLs — and was reportedly close to re-signing with the Giants before the Titans swooped in with a 3-year, $45M deal ($32M guaranteed). His loss creates a significant hole at CB2 opposite Paulson Adebo. The Giants have Deonte Banks but no proven starter-level replacement currently signed.

Released by the Giants as a cap casualty to create ~$9M in savings. Okereke was a starter who finished 2025 with 143 tackles (4th-most in franchise history) but the team identified his contract as expendable given the LB class depth and their desire to upgrade. Giants immediately replaced him in free agency with Tremaine Edmunds.

Bellinger signed a 3-year, $24M deal with the Titans. His role had diminished with the emergence of Theo Johnson and was further reduced by the Giants' signing of Isaiah Likely. A planned departure rather than a surprise. Chris Manhertz re-signed to continue in the blocking TE role.

Signed a 1-year deal with the Titans. Schlottmann was a versatile backup capable of playing center and guard. His departure leaves the Giants without a backup center under contract, exacerbating the IOL depth concern.
Signed a 1-year deal with the Jets. Was the Giants' third safety and special teams contributor. Never carved out a starting role. Replaced in part by Ar'Darius Washington signing.
Released as a cap casualty to make way for Jordan Stout signing. Gillan was among the worst punters in the league by net average (36.9 net, 30th in NFL) in 2025. Pure upgrade situation.
Released after an ineffective tenure. Had a severe on-field meltdown in Week 2 of 2025 that rendered him untrustworthy. Marcus Mbow passed him on the depth chart. Low-impact departure.

signings.contract_notes: Base value $40M with upside to $47.5M via incentives per Jordan Schultz/The Athletic. Guaranteed amount not officially confirmed; estimated at ~$23M based on market context and Spotrac data. Makes Likely the 3rd-highest paid TE in the NFL at signing.
signings.analysis: A scheme-defining move for John Harbaugh's first offseason. Likely, 25, reunites with Harbaugh who coached him for four seasons in Baltimore. He is the clear starter at TE over Theo Johnson, who remains a significant contributor. With Likely and Johnson, the Giants are signaling a 12-personnel heavy offensive identity. At $13.3M AAV, this is a real starter-level investment — not a bridge move. TE need is fully addressed for the foreseeable future; they do not need to draft a TE high.

The Giants released Bobby Okereke to clear cap space and immediately upgraded the position by signing Edmunds to a 3-year, $36M deal with $23.7M fully guaranteed. Edmunds, 27, is a 2x Pro Bowler with 900 career tackles, 14 INTs, and 100+ tackles in every full season played. At $12M AAV with $23.7M guaranteed, this is a true starter investment — the full guaranteed window signals the Giants view him as their long-term LB1. The interior LB need that escalated with Okereke's release is fully resolved. Drafting a developmental LB remains possible but is no longer a priority.

Retaining Eluemunor was the most critical offensive line move of the offseason. At 3-year/$39M ($26M guaranteed), the Giants keep their starting bookend tackles — Andrew Thomas on the left, Eluemunor on the right — intact heading into Jaxson Dart's sophomore season. Eluemunor played 1,000+ snaps in 2025 for the first time in his career and helped power a top-5 rushing offense. At $13M AAV, this is below the $20M+ elite OT market — a genuine bargain for a proven starter. RT is now a non-need. The Giants can allow Marcus Mbow to develop as the swing tackle.

signings.contract_notes: Guaranteed amount not officially confirmed; estimated. Makes Stout the highest-paid punter in NFL history per Adam Schefter.
signings.analysis: Pure position upgrade over the released Jamie Gillan. Stout was the 2025 first-team All-Pro punter, leading the NFL with a 44.9 net punting average. Gillan ranked 30th in net average. Part of the Harbaugh culture import from Baltimore, where special teams is treated as a core priority. Does not move any pre-existing positional need — this was a specialists overhaul, not a roster hole fill.

signings.contract_notes: Guaranteed amount estimated; 1-year deal worth $3M per Connor Hughes/SNY.
signings.analysis: Another Harbaugh-to-Giants pipeline move. Washington, 26, is a versatile DB who can play slot corner, free safety, and box safety, and contributes heavily on special teams. He's coming off a torn Achilles that cost him most of 2025. On a 1-year/$3M deal, this is a prove-it/depth signing that partially addresses the third safety hole left by Dane Belton's departure to the Jets. Does NOT solve any starting-level secondary need — CB2 hole from Flott's departure remains unaddressed by this signing.

signings.contract_notes: Terms not officially disclosed; estimated minimum-adjacent 1-year deal per multiple reports.
signings.analysis: Blocking TE retained on a 1-year deal. Manhertz played all 17 games in 2025 and is an integral part of the run game as the in-line blocking TE in Harbaugh's anticipated 12-personnel sets. Low cost, clear role. No impact on any positional need — he's a complementary piece, not a starter.

signings.contract_notes: Terms not officially confirmed; re-signed prior to the start of the negotiating window as an exclusive rights or minimum-adjacent deal.
signings.analysis: Olszewski returns on a 1-year deal as a trustworthy return specialist and slot depth option. He filled in capably for Wan'Dale Robinson at slot receiver last season. With Robinson gone to Tennessee, Olszewski now projects as the primary slot competition heading into training camp. The 1-year deal signals he is a bridge piece, not a long-term answer. WR (slot specifically) remains an active need despite this signing.

signings.contract_notes: Terms not officially confirmed; 1-year deal per ESPN. Fits the physical, blocking receiver profile Harbaugh prefers.
signings.analysis: Hodgins re-signs on a 1-year deal after returning to the Giants midway through 2025 and making 5 starts. He fits the Harbaugh offensive identity — a physical, blocking-capable receiver who can line up outside. He provides depth and competition but is not a starter-level solution. The WR2/slot need created by Robinson's departure is NOT addressed by this signing. Giants still need a true WR2.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Eluemunor re-signed on a 3-year/$39M deal ($26M guaranteed) — a real investment that keeps the bookend tackle situation with Andrew Thomas intact. Marcus Mbow continues developing as swing tackle. RT is not a draft need. If they draft an OT, it's purely developmental day 3.

The Okereke release escalated this need temporarily, but the Edmunds signing (3-year/$36M, $23.7M fully guaranteed) immediately neutralized it. Edmunds is a 2x Pro Bowler at 27 in his prime. LB is no longer a premium draft need. A rotational/developmental LB in the middle rounds is possible but not urgent.

Isaiah Likely (3-year/$40M) is a full-time starter investment at TE. With Theo Johnson as a proven second TE and Manhertz retained as the blocking specialist, this position is fully stacked. The Giants should not draft a TE in any meaningful round.

Cor'Dale Flott's departure to the Titans (3-year/$45M) is the biggest need-creating loss of the offseason. The Giants now have Paulson Adebo at CB1 and Deonte Banks as a candidate for CB2, but no proven starter at CB2 under contract. Ar'Darius Washington (1-year/$3M) is a depth/slot piece, not a CB2 answer. CB has escalated to the team's most urgent draft/remaining FA need. Should be priority in Round 1 or 2 of the draft, or via a mid-tier free agent signing.

Wan'Dale Robinson's departure to the Titans (4-year/$78M) creates a slot/WR2 void that is not adequately addressed by the Hodgins or Olszewski re-signings — both are on 1-year depth deals. Malik Nabers (returning from ACL) is the clear WR1, but the WR2/slot role remains a live need. The Giants may address this in the second wave of FA or the draft. Need is unchanged from pre-FA level, though it was expected Robinson would leave.

Greg Van Roten remains unsigned and the Giants have no confirmed starting-caliber guard added. Austin Schlottmann's departure to the Titans also removes their backup center depth. John Michael Schmitz Jr. is the starting center under contract. The RG spot is the primary IOL need entering the draft. No FA addition has moved this needle.
No edge-related signings or departures of note. Dexter Lawrence anchors the interior. The Giants' edge depth remains a concern with no significant addition. Need unchanged.
No significant IDL additions or departures. Dexter Lawrence is the anchor. No change in need tier.

Tyler Nubin and Jevon Holland project as the starting safety duo. Ar'Darius Washington adds depth on a 1-year deal but is injury-prone (torn Achilles in 2025) and is not a locked-in starter. Dane Belton's departure to the Jets is a marginal loss. Safety is not a priority need but remains a depth concern.

key_additions.headline: Harbaugh reunites with his former Ravens TE to anchor a 12-personnel offensive scheme around Jaxson Dart
Two-time Pro Bowl linebacker upgrades the defense's green dot role, replacing the released Bobby Okereke
Re-signed right tackle keeps the starting bookend duo with Andrew Thomas intact to protect Dart
2025 All-Pro punter (50.1 gross avg, 44.9 net avg — 1st in NFL) becomes NFL's highest-paid punter, replacing Jamie Gillan
Versatile Harbaugh-system DB adds secondary depth and special teams value coming off torn Achilles
Return specialist and slot depth re-signed to compete for vacated slot role after Robinson's departure
Physical blocking receiver re-signed as WR depth in new Harbaugh offense
Blocking TE retained to anchor the running game in anticipated 12-personnel sets

key_losses.headline: Slot receiver who hit 1,000 yards in 2025 departs for Tennessee, leaving a significant WR2 void
Starting CB2 who posted 11 PDs in 2025 bolted for Tennessee after a last-minute Titans pitch, creating the team's biggest roster hole
Three-year Giants starter released as cap casualty to fund Tremaine Edmunds upgrade
Backup TE departs to Tennessee following the Giants' signing of Isaiah Likely
Versatile backup C/G departs, leaving the Giants without a backup center under contract
Third safety and special teams contributor moves crosstown to the Jets
